channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Decoupling Temporal Services with Nexus and the Java SDK
item.description: Your Temporal services share a blast radius. A bug in Compliance at 3 AM crashes Payments, too, because they share the same Worker. The obvious fix is separate services with HTTP calls between...

item.title: 7 Real Workflows That Actually Save Developers Hours
item.description: Most developers are still using AI like a novelty keyboard shortcut. They ask it for regexes, toy apps, or random refactors, then wonder why the payoff feels small. That is the wrong mental model....

item.title: Platform Engineering: Building Internal Developer Platforms
item.description: Introduction Developers at top tech companies deploy code with a simple git push. They provision databases with a Slack command. They view production metrics in seconds. Meanwhile, at many...

item.title: The Login Portal That Actively Hates You
item.description: Submission for the DEV April Fools Challenge 🎭 What I Built I built a Premium Secure Portal — a login form that uses advanced Anti-UX patterns to guarantee that no user, human or bot, can ever...

item.title: Optimizing FFmpeg for Production: Settings That Cut Processing Time by 40%
item.description: FFmpeg's default settings are designed for output quality, not processing speed. On a production server where you're processing dozens of videos per hour, the difference between the right settings...

item.title: How to Download and Upload Large Models with the Hugging Face CLI
item.description: Managing large model files on Hugging Face can feel cumbersome — especially when dealing with multigigabyte embeddings or generative models. Fortunately, the Hugging Face CLI provides a...

item.title: On-Device LLM Inference via KMP and llama.cpp

item.title: Google Workspace Studio Tutorial: Auto-Organize Your Inbox with Smart Labels & Priority Notifications
item.description: The average professional spends 28% of their workday reading and answering emails. But here's the problem, not all emails are created equal. A Pizza Discount doesn't deserve the same mental space...

item.title: Your AI Coding Tool Is Not Electricity. Stop Treating It Like a Utility.
item.description: Anthropic's Thariq just announced an adjustment to Claude Code session limits during peak hours. Not weekly limits (unchanged), just how fast you burn through sessions between 5am and 11am PT. 7%...

item.title: What the Claude Code Leak Reveals: The Engine Isn't the Moat — The Harness Is
item.description: On March 31, 2026, security researcher Chaofan Shou discovered something Anthropic probably didn't want the world to see: the entire source code of Claude Code — Anthropic's official AI coding CLI...

item.title: This even smaller credit card-sized e-reader has one tragic flaw
item.description: There’s a new smallest e-reader in my life. The Xteink X3 is even smaller and thinner than Xteink’s X4 while also improving on the X4 with refined (but still far from great) software, simplified...

item.title: Google Home’s latest update makes Gemini better at understanding your commands
item.description: Google is launching another update to its Home app, which is supposed to make controlling your smart home with its Gemini AI assistant "more natural and reliable," according to this week's release...

item.title: United adds TSA wait times to its mobile app
item.description: If you're flying United Airlines, you'll now have a better idea of when you need to get to the airport to make your flight. Yesterday the airline announced several updates to its iOS and Android...

item.title: Rising gas prices are good news for EV sales, for now
item.description: It's become an American truism: Once you buy an SUV, there's no going back. And our love affair with pickups has been documented in a million country songs and TV commercials. But a brutal spike...

item.title: Transfer Point is a modern adventure game made with 40-year-old software
item.description: One of the year's most intriguing games was developed using software first released 40 years ago. Transfer Point looks and plays like a classic Mac point-and-click adventure game, and there's a...

item.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next. As the war...

item.title: Researchers Uncover Mining Operation Using ISO Lures to Spread RATs and Crypto Miners
item.description: A financially motivated operation codenamed REF1695 has been observed leveraging fake installers to deploy remote access trojans (RATs) and cryptocurrency miners since November 2023. "Beyond...

item.title: The State of Trusted Open Source Report
item.description: In December 2025, we shared the first-ever The State of Trusted Open Source report, featuring insights from our product data and customer base on open source consumption across our catalog of...

item.title: Uber expands its $4,000 ‘Go Electric’ grant to drivers nationwide
item.description: Since last year, Uber has been offering drivers in a handful of locations - California, Colorado, Massachusetts and New York City - $4,000 to swap their gas guzzlers for electric vehicles. The...

item.title: Insider trading or random guy? It doesn’t matter to Polymarket
item.description: In mid-March, conspiracy theories swirled claiming Benjamin Netanyahu had been replaced by an AI clone. Though there was no actual proof that the Israeli Prime Minister had been injured or killed,...

item.title: Possible US Government iPhone Hacking Tool Leaked
item.description: Wired writes (alternate source): Security researchers at Google on Tuesday released a report describing what they’re calling “Coruna,” a highly sophisticated iPhone hacking toolkit that includes...

item.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
item.description: As the war in Iran continues to engulf the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz stays closed, one of the most visible global economic ripple effects has been fossil-fuel prices. In particular, you...

item.title: WhatsApp Alerts 200 Users After Fake iOS App Installed Spyware; Italian Firm Faces Action
item.description: Meta-owned messaging platform WhatsApp said it alerted about 200 users who were tricked into installing a bogus version of its iOS app that was infected with spyware. According to reports from...

item.title: Apple Expands iOS 18.7.7 Update to More Devices to Block DarkSword Exploit
item.description: Apple on Wednesday expanded the availability of iOS 18.7.7 and iPadOS 18.7.7 to a broader range of devices to protect users from the risk posed by a recently disclosed exploit kit known as...

item.title: Trump’s birthright citizenship ban may fail — but the administration already got too far
item.description: On Wednesday morning, the Supreme Court heard arguments in Trump v. Barbara, a case challenging President Donald Trump's 2025 executive order banning birthright citizenship. Justices seemed...

item.title: NASA launches four astronauts toward the Moon on the Artemis II mission
item.description: NASA's Artemis II flight, which is set to take four astronauts toward the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years, successfully launched on Wednesday evening. The Artemis II mission, part of...

item.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
item.description: Anthropic executives said it was an accident and retracted the bulk of the takedown notices.

item.title: The Artemis Moon base project is legally dubious
item.description: With NASA planning to launch four astronauts on Wednesday on its Artemis II mission, the race to return to the Moon is back on. The current mission will see astronauts aboard the Orion capsule...

item.title: Meta’s natural gas binge could power South Dakota
item.description: Meta's upcoming Hyperion AI data center will be powered by 10 new natural gas plants.

item.title: AI Models Lie, Cheat, and Steal to Protect Other Models From Being Deleted
item.description: A new study from researchers at UC Berkeley and UC Santa Cruz suggests models will disobey human commands to protect their own kind.
